title: digitalis cariensis subsp. ikarica
intro: Digitalis cariensis subsp. ikarica is a plant species within the Digitalis genus, commonly referred to as a type of foxglove. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its cardiac glycosides which have been found to exhibit cardiotonic activity, helping to regulate the heart's rhythm and increase its contractions. The health benefits of Digitalis cariensis subsp. ikarica include its potential to treat conditions such as atrial fibrillation, congestive heart failure, and other heart-related issues. In herbal preparations, Digitalis cariensis subsp. ikarica is often dried and used to make teas, tinctures, or infusions, although it is essential to consult a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment for any medical condition.
